While geopolitical tensions offer support, rising crude prices and delayed US Fed rate cuts suggest only a limited recovery rather than a fresh breakaway rally for the metal.

title: FIIs back 11 stocks for 3 straight quarters of FY26; 7 turn multibaggers
summary: Foreign institutional investors have steadily raised stakes in several Indian companies through FY26, signalling conviction despite market volatility. Eleven such stocks delivered returns between 70% and 1,500%, with seven turning multibaggers. The trend highlights how tracking FII activity can help identify outperformers even during broader market corrections.

title: Stock market crash: Over 1,500 stocks hit 52-week lows as Sensex tanks 1,636 points
summary: As many as 1,546 stocks, including TCS, ITC, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Bajaj Finserv, and Trent, hit their 52-week lows on the BSE during the session.

title: How do excise cuts change India’s oil sector math?
summary: While the cuts provide a critical buffer for fuel retailers, the real winners emerge in the upstream segment as producers escape the windfall tax net.

title: Top Gainers & Losers on Mar 30: Hindustan Copper, Tata Motors, Ceat, OLA, Canara Bank, Paytm among top losers
summary: The Indian stock market continued its decline on March 30, with the Nifty 50 and S&amp;P BSE Sensex dropping 2% each, marking their worst monthly decline in six years at over 11%. Sector indices like Nifty PSU Bank fell 4.5%, reflecting widespread selling pressure.

title: Rupee's worst performance since 2013 taper tantrum: How worrying is the decline?
summary: The rupee faces its worst decline since the 2013 ‘taper tantrum’ as the RBI shifts from aggressive defense to calibrated depreciation. While emergency curbs offer temporary relief, structural issues and energy shocks continue to challenge India’s $700 billion war chest.

title: Indian rupee slips past 95 mark for first time amid escalating US-Iran war. Can it touch 100 per dollar?
summary: USD vs INR: The rupee weakened past the 95 per dollar mark for the first time to 95.20 per dollar, down 0.3% on the day. Check out what experts say on the near-term outlook of the domestic currency.

title: Urban Company shares jump 8% as InstaHelp crosses 1 million bookings in March
summary: Urban Company experienced an 8% stock increase on March 30, driven by the success of its InstaHelp service, which surpassed 1 million bookings. While the stock has rebounded 12% this month, it remains significantly lower than its peak value.

title: Bank locker loss? Why RBI rules have capped the compensation limit — Explained
summary: Banks provide locker holders compensation fixed at 100 times the annual locker rent in case of loss, Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman said while replying to a question in the Lok Sabha.

title: War or no war, the fundamentals of investing remain unchanged: Sanjay Grover of Baroda BNP Paribas Mutual Fund
summary: For long-term investors, staggered investments through diversified equity funds remain a prudent way to navigate volatility, says Sanjay Grover, MD and CEO, Baroda BNP Paribas Mutual Fund.

title: What are FD-backed credit cards? Are they worth it? Benefits, risks and best options
summary: FD-backed credit cards offer a simple way to build a credit score using fixed deposits, with easy approval, steady interest earnings, and manageable risks when used responsibly and strategically. Here is a list of the top credit cards against fixed deposits in 2026.

title: Bajaj Finserv, HDFC Bank among 7 Sensex stocks that hit 52-week lows; slip up to 18% in a month
Stock market crash: RIL to SBI— Magnificent 7 stocks in India lose  ₹3.28 lakh crore in 2 days as Nifty tanks 4%
summary: Stock market crash: HDFC Bank, Reliance Industries, ICICI Bank, Bharti Airtel, Infosys, SBI and Larsen &amp; Toubro (L&amp;T) together form the Magnificent 7 stocks in India, and have lost  ₹328,702 crore in just two sessions.

title: Quote of the day by Bhavish Aggarwal: ‘The future is not something you predict, it's something…’
summary: Bhavish Aggarwal’s quote urges people to actively shape their future through initiative and action rather than waiting passively. It emphasizes ownership, consistent effort, and turning ideas into reality, inspiring a proactive mindset to create meaningful change.

title: Achieve FY27 personal finance goals: Here's a month-by-month guide to beat deadlines, manage taxes and investments
summary: A structured personal finance plan for FY27 helps manage taxes, investments and spending with ease, ensuring disciplined money management, timely compliance and steady long-term financial growth.

title: The unplanned expense in your 50s: Paying for ageing parents’ care
summary: Geriatric care mainly has two recurring expenses: round the clock care and medical costs.&nbsp;

title: No one knows which asset class will do well. Nithin Kamath tells what 99% investors should do
summary: Nithin Kamath says predicting winning asset classes is futile, urging investors to diversify and stay invested long-term amid volatility. Weak markets, foreign outflows, rising oil prices and currency pressures are keeping sentiment fragile and downside risks elevated in near term.

title: Weekly Top Picks: These stocks scored 10 on 10 on Stock Reports Plus
summary: Stock Reports Plus, powered by Refinitiv, undertakes detailed company analysis for 4,000+ listed stocks. In addition to detailed company analyses, the report also collates analysts’ forecasts and trend analysis for each component. An average score in Stock Reports Plus is calculated by undertaking quantitative analysis of five key investment tools – earnings, fundamentals, relative valuation, risk, and price momentum

title: Investors lose Rs 1.34 lakh cr in FY26 as Sensex slumps 7%, Nifty ends 5% lower; PSU banks shine
summary: Indian markets ended FY26 in the red amid global uncertainty and domestic pressures, with Nifty falling over 5% and Sensex declining 7%. Persistent foreign outflows, weak earnings, high valuations, and a weakening rupee kept sentiment subdued, while escalating tensions involving Iran further worsened the outlook by driving up energy prices and dampening hopes of US rate cuts.

title: STT hike on F&O kicks in from April 1: Will it worsen pain for traders amid US-Iran war-led volatility?
summary: STT Hike: The government announced an increase in STT on futures contracts to 0.05% from 0.02%. STT on options premium and exercise of options will rise to 0.15% from the present rate of 0.1% and 0.125%, respectively.

title: Risk-off has reset India’s EM positioning; a broad rerating is premature
summary: India remains on the FII sell list alongside other emerging markets; while selling has moderated week-on-week, it is too early to conclude that the trend has turned positive.

title: US stock market today: Dow, S&P 500 futures gain even as US-Iran war drags into fifth week
summary: After four weeks of declines, US stock futures indicate a positive start on March 30, despite escalating Middle East tensions. Rising oil prices are raising inflation concerns, contributing to the largest monthly drop in over three years.

title: Dr. Reddy's can supply leftover Olymviq stock to government hospitals after 30-day window
summary: The Delhi high court's ruling ensures crucial semaglutide supply for diabetics, allowing Dr. Reddy's to clear its existing inventory to public healthcare facilities.

title: Cement to get dearer as Iran war pinches manufacturers
summary: India’s cement leaders are set to hike prices in April to offset a massive spike in fuel and packaging costs driven by the war in West Asia. While the increases aim to protect margins, they threaten to raise construction costs.

title: CoinDCX co-founders plan next steps after ‘shocking and disheartening’ scam accusation, promise safety infrastructure
summary: Swept up in the alleged over  ₹71 lakh fraud case. CoinDCX's Sumit Gupta announced that the company is focused on safety and has committed  ₹100 crore towards development of the infrastructure.

title: JDA and capital gains: What happens when family members co-own property
summary: When a property is settled among family members before a joint development deal, can capital gains be fully exempt if each retains a flat? Here’s how Sections 82 and 86 apply.

title: Home loan after 60: 5 ways pensioners can boost credit score, improve approval chances
summary: Improving credit score after 60 can ease home loan approvals, with simple steps like timely repayments, and joint applications helping pensioners secure better loan terms and rates. This can be accomplished successfully, with proper planning and guidance from a certified financial advisor.&nbsp;
